commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of an intentional availability tradeoff and overlap coordination, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: The diff changes local cron timeout policy and tests only; it does not touch credentials, permissions, dependency sources, workflows, package metadata, or code download paths. (sha=f5f3d7c8ed9a439dfc93eeaf3b43af714d2d504e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes cron timeout resolution so main-session `systemEvent` jobs with `wakeMode: "now"` use the existing 60-minute agent-turn safety ceiling, with focused policy and timer regression tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +78. Total +88 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main gives every non-agentTurn payload the 10-minute default while the main `wakeMode: "now"` path awaits `runHeartbeatOnce`. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of an intentional availability tradeoff and overlap coordination, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(cron): extend main system event timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Extend the cron outer watchdog only for main-session `systemEvent` jobs with `wakeMode: "now"`.
- Preserve the existing 10-minute default for other `systemEvent` jobs, including `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"`.
- Preserve existing `agentTurn.timeoutSeconds` behavior and the existing 60-minute safety ceiling for agent-turn jobs without an explicit timeout.

## AI assistance disclosure

I used Codex to inspect the issue, related closed PRs, existing cron source, tests, and validation output, then to draft and verify this patch. I reviewed and understand the affected path: cron resolves the job timeout in `src/cron/service/timeout-policy.ts`, `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` applies that watchdog in `src/cron/service/timer.ts`, and main-session wake-now system events block while awaiting `runHeartbeatOnce`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A blocking main-session cron `systemEvent` with `wakeMode: "now"` no longer uses the generic 10-minute job timeout while cron is waiting for `runHeartbeatOnce`; the next-heartbeat systemEvent path remains on the generic timeout.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw workspace on the rebased branch using the real cron timeout resolver and `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` path with isolated fake cron dependencies and no live credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/issue-50621/runtime-proof.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
issue_50621_runtime_proof
target_job=sessionTarget:main payload:systemEvent wakeMode:now
default_system_event_timeout_ms=600000
agent_turn_safety_timeout_ms=3600000
resolved_target_timeout_ms=3600000
resolved_next_heartbeat_timeout_ms=600000
watchdog_delay_ms=3600000
settled_after_default_window=false
run_heartbeat_once_calls=1
final_status=ok
final_summary=long main-session task
proof_passed=true
proof_status=0
```

Observed result after fix: The target main-session wake-now systemEvent resolved to the 60-minute safety ceiling, did not settle after the old 10-minute window, called `runHeartbeatOnce` once, and completed successfully after the heartbeat promise resolved. A main-session next-heartbeat systemEvent still resolved to the 10-minute default.

What was not tested: A live provider/model run was not exercised; the proof isolates the cron watchdog path rather than spending credentials or model time.

## Root Cause

`resolveCronJobTimeoutMs` treated every `systemEvent` job as generic cron work, so it returned `DEFAULT_JOB_TIMEOUT_MS` even when the main-session wake-now path synchronously awaited `runHeartbeatOnce`. That made a long but valid main-session heartbeat run fail around the generic cron watchdog window even though the underlying work was running in the main session.

## Dependency contract / source proof

No external dependency behavior is changed. The relevant internal contracts are:

- `src/cron/types.ts`: `timeoutSeconds` belongs to `agentTurn` payloads, not `systemEvent` payloads.
- `src/cron/service/jobs.ts`: main-session jobs are `systemEvent` payloads; isolated/current/session target jobs are `agentTurn` payloads.
- `src/cron/service/timer.ts`: `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` wraps the actual run using `resolveCronJobTimeoutMs`, and the blocking main-session path awaits `runHeartbeatOnce` only for `wakeMode: "now"`.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/cron/service/timeout-policy.test.ts` covers the exact policy split:
  - main `systemEvent` + `wakeMode: "now"` uses `AGENT_TURN_SAFETY_TIMEOUT_MS`.
  - main `systemEvent` + `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"` keeps `DEFAULT_JOB_TIMEOUT_MS`.
  - existing agent-turn explicit/no-timeout behavior remains unchanged.
- `src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` covers the real wrapper path by keeping `runHeartbeatOnce` pending past the old default timeout and confirming the job does not complete early.

## Security Impact

No credential, network, auth, or permission behavior changes. The patch only adjusts a local cron watchdog ceiling for one already-supported blocking runtime path.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the affected cron timeout path, the main-session wake-now execution path, the related issue discussion, and closed reference PRs before accepting the final patch shape.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

The touched files are not matched by `.github/CODEOWNERS`. Nearby cron owner rules exist for `src/cron/stagger.ts` and `src/cron/service/jobs.ts`, but this patch does not touch either file.

## Changelog

Contributor PR: no `CHANGELOG.md` entry added.

## Review conversations

Reference issue/PR review checked before implementation:

- Issue #50621 and duplicate #70325.
- Closed narrow attempts #50670, #50698, and #50723.
- Closed broader/configurable watchdog attempt #83066 as a shape to avoid.
- No open PR currently linked to or substantially addressing #50621 was found.

## Risks

- Main-session wake-now cron jobs can occupy the cron run slot longer than before, up to the existing 60-minute safety ceiling.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- This keeps bounded failure behavior instead of removing the timeout or making all systemEvent jobs unbounded.
- It gives the blocking path the same ceiling already used for agent-turn-style long work while leaving enqueue-only systemEvent modes short.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"` behavior.
- Does not add configuration.
- Does not make all `systemEvent` jobs unbounded or long-running.
- Does not change `agentTurn.timeoutSeconds`, including `timeoutSeconds <= 0`.
- Does not change heartbeat scheduling, delivery, or provider/model timeout behavior.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timeout-policy.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts
```

Passed: 3 files, 58 tests.

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
```

Passed: changed lanes `core`, `coreTests`.

```bash
codex review --base origin/main
```

Passed: "The timeout policy change is narrowly scoped to main-session wake-now systemEvent jobs and is covered by focused policy and regression tests. I did not identify a correctness regression in the changed code."
target.author: ferminquant

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
